title: Member selects and hierarchical names
body:
According to [this answer](https://stackoverflow.com/a/34816930) , I can use dot to access member variables of a module. However, I got errors below when I use it.

```
sim_1/new/28F640P30.v:2737: syntax error
sim_1/new/28F640P30.v:2738: Syntax in assignment statement l-value.
sim_1/new/28F640P30.v:3164: syntax error
sim_1/new/28F640P30.v:3164: error: malformed statement
sim_1/new/28F640P30.v:3165: syntax error
sim_1/new/28F640P30.v:3165: error: malformed statement
...
```

Example:

```verilog
#TimingData_man.tVDHPH
```

Full codes are placed [here](https://paste.ubuntu.com/p/4SZP6GQsgk/) and [here (header file containing the definition of module)](https://paste.ubuntu.com/p/KRS2ztHT2w/) .

sim_1/new/28F640P30.v:2737: syntax error

sim_1/new/28F640P30.v:3164: error: malformed statement
sim_1/new/28F640P30.v:3165: syntax error
